q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is JSONResponse on line 27, which constructs an HTTP response object without performing network I/O, shell execution, or other dangerous side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The violation (exposed exception message and class name) is embedded in the returned JSONResponse, observable to external users via the HTTP response.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' with exprs containing the JSONResponse construction, so the sensitive data is directly accessible in the returned object in memory.
